artifact.name: Operatorology Existence Framework (English)

flow.meaning: Continuous-time existence flow; fixed points satisfy 𝒰x = x.

projection.meaning: Projection to invariant existence state (when the limit exists).

reality_set.meaning: Reality defined as the zero-set of the existence metric.

meta_existence.meaning: A law/operator exists (is stable) when it is a fixed point in operator-space.

definitions.name: State Space
definitions.text: The set of possible configurations (states) x.

definitions.name: Existence Operator
definitions.text: A transformation 𝒰 : 𝕊 → 𝕊 describing intrinsic evolution.

definitions.name: Deviation
definitions.text: Δ(x) = 𝒰x − x (requires a state difference operator).

definitions.name: Deviation Operator
definitions.text: 𝒟 = 𝒰 − 𝕀, with Existence = ker(𝒟).

artifact.minimal_summary_for_poster: Existence is an invariant solution of evolution. A state x exists stably iff 𝒰x = x. Define the existence metric 𝓜(x) = ‖𝒰x − x‖; reality is the zero-set 𝓜(x)=0. All stable structures—particles, memories, intelligences—are elements of ker(𝒰−𝕀).
